Yeah, so the TLDR should go at the top of pages, so they're not in and of themselves, specific pages, and then the goal is that essentially Google is prioritizing answers within the first 30% of any page, so when Google like, is searching for answers to intent, it really wants it to be like, very self-evident, so

For pages where you eventually get to the intent four headers down because you're doing the what is blah blah blah that a human needs, the TLDRs create a bite-sized answer to the search intent high enough up on the page that the page is still seen as productive or useful without interfering with the human experience of if someone does land on a blog and decide to read all 3,000 words of it.
